basics.name: Shuo Yang
basics.summary: Undergraduate student in Microelectronics at Shanghai Jiao Tong University and research assistant at the ICRG Lab, focusing on near-memory computing architecture optimization and hardware-software co-design.

work.name: ICRG Lab
work.summary: Conducting research on high-performance computing architectures, near-memory computing, and hardware-software co-design.
highlights: Working on architecture optimization for vector retrieval, long-context inference, and model security workloads.

publications.name: NasZip: NDP-Accelerated Approximate Nearest Neighbor Retrieval Framework
publications.summary: An NDP-accelerated ANNS framework for RAG systems, co-first author.

publications.name: EstAtt: Linear-Complexity Attention via Sign-Based Estimation
publications.summary: A sign-based estimation framework for linear-complexity attention.

presentations.name: NasZip: NDP-Accelerated ANNS for RAG Systems
presentations.description: Hardware-software co-optimization research for ANNS in RAG systems.
